title: Korean Gochujang Chicken Rice Bowl

ingredients.name: boneless chicken (thigh or breast), bite-size pieces

subs.name: 500 g paneer or soya chunks — marinate and cook the same way for a vegetarian version
2 tbsp red chilli paste + 1 tsp soy sauce + 1 tsp vinegar — a passable stand-in
jaggery dissolved in 1 tbsp warm water, or plain sugar — same sticky sweetness

steps.text: Mix gochujang, soy sauce, honey, minced garlic, grated ginger and gingelly oil into a thick marinade. Toss the chicken pieces in this and rest for 10 minutes at room temperature.
Cook rice with 2.5 cups water: 2 whistles in a pressure cooker or 18 minutes covered on a saucepan over low-medium heat. Fluff with a fork and keep warm.

Heat oil in a wide kadai over medium-high flame. Spread the chicken in a single layer and sear without stirring for 2–3 minutes until the underside is charred and the gochujang sauce has caramelised slightly.

Toss the chicken and cook for 5–6 more minutes until fully cooked through and coated in a sticky, deeply coloured glaze. If the pan dries out, add a splash of water and scrape up the caramelised bits from the base.

Divide the warm rice into bowls. Spoon the gochujang chicken on top with all its sticky glaze. Scatter spring onion greens and sesame seeds over everything, drizzle a few drops of gingelly oil, and serve immediately.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chicken → 500 g paneer or soya chunks — marinate and cook the same way for a vegetarian version; honey → jaggery dissolved in 1 tbsp warm water, or plain sugar — same sticky sweetness; gochujang → 2 tbsp red chilli paste + 1 tsp soy sauce + 1 tsp vinegar — a passable stand-in
indian_kitchen_adaptation: This bowl is seamlessly made in an Indian kitchen. A kadai handles the sticky gochujang glaze better than a thin non-stick pan — the stainless steel or cast-iron base encourages caramelisation. Cook rice in a pressure cooker for 2 whistles as you would for pulao. Gochujang is stocked in specialty sections of metro supermarkets; if unavailable, combine 2 tbsp red chilli paste with 1 tsp soy sauce and 1 tsp sugar. Honey can be replaced with jaggery dissolved in a teaspoon of warm water.
